coin_profile.name: Ethereum
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.
coin_profile.description: Ethereum is the leading smart contract platform and the foundation of decentralised finance (DeFi). It transitioned to proof-of-stake in 2022 (The Merge), making it energy-efficient. ETH is used to pay for computation on the network and is deflationary post-EIP-1559, with a portion of fees burned with every transaction. It underpins the majority of DeFi, NFT, and Layer 2 ecosystems.

ETH.name: Ethereum

moat.rationale: Ethereum commands an exceptional moat as the foundational settlement layer for DeFi and Web3, with 15+ years of proven security, immense ecosystem lock-in across thousands of protocols and billions in TVL, and near-perfect token utility through staking rewards and fee capture. While technically forkable and facing regulatory headwinds, the combination of network effects (all major dApps depend on ETH), capital efficiency (9/10), dilution control (10/10), and protocol maturity (10/10) creates defensibility rivaled only by Bitcoin. The primary risk is regulatory pressure on proof-of-stake validators, but decentralization partially mitigates this—unlike centralized L1 competitors.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.

Bear (-12.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. Unlike stocks, crypto has no analyst EPS forecasts. These rates are based purely on quality and network strength — treat them as a rough guide, not a precise prediction.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Holder Revenue produced a value below 10% of current price (fees are low relative to market cap) — shown in the breakdown chart but excluded from the median pool. Network Activity (NVT) treats 24h trading volume as a proxy for on-chain utility — it fires when volume is available and the resulting IV falls within 0.1x–10x of current price. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

coin_profile.name: Ethereum Classic
coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Store of Value asset is a cryptocurrency whose main purpose is to hold and protect your wealth over time — the same way people have used gold for thousands of years. It does not earn fees from users. Instead, its value comes from scarcity, trust, and the belief that it will be worth more in the future. Bitcoin is the most well-known example. We value these assets by comparing them to gold and looking at the cost of producing new coins.
coin_profile.description: Ethereum Classic is the original Ethereum chain that continued after the 2016 DAO hack fork. It maintains the original 'code is law' philosophy and is proof-of-work. ETC has a fixed supply schedule and positions itself as a store of value for the Ethereum ecosystem.

forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like digital gold. It does not earn fees — its value comes from people trusting it as a safe place to store wealth, the same way people have trusted gold for centuries. We use two checks: (1) How much of gold's total value has this asset captured so far? If that share grows, the price goes up. (2) How much does it cost to produce one coin? Prices rarely stay below production cost for long because miners would stop mining, reducing supply. The Speculative Fair Value is the average of both checks.

forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket B (Store of Value): Up to 6 methods are available — Monetary Premium, Production Cost Floor, Metcalfe Network Value, Supply Adjusted Value, Network Activity (NVT), and Rank Benchmark. Monetary Premium measures the token's current share of gold's market cap, projecting fair value if that share is maintained as gold appreciates. Production Cost Floor is unavailable: this token is not a PoW-mineable asset or mining cost data is not available. Supply Adjusted Value penalises the large gap between circulating and fully-diluted supply — inconsistent with a credible scarcity claim. Network Activity (NVT) treats volume as monetary circulation velocity — higher NVT targets apply to SoV assets held long-term rather than traded for fees. Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.

<cite index="1-12">Long-term success hinges on differentiating from centralized alternatives in the MiCA-compliant stablecoin landscape</cite>, creating material execution risk if institutional adoption favors centralized issuers or central bank digital currencies over decentralized protocols.

coin_profile.bucket_explanation: A Cash-Flow Protocol is a blockchain or app that earns real money from its users — just like a business. Every time someone makes a trade, borrows money, or uses the network, it collects a small fee. The more people use it, the more fees it earns. We value these tokens similarly to how you would value a business — by looking at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it.

forecast_meta.growth_basis_note: Annual growth rates derived from quality score 3.6/10 and survivability 40.0%. Base: 3.0% p.a., Bull: 9.0% p.a., Bear: -37.0% p.a.
forecast_meta.method_explanation: How we valued this token: We treat this like a small business. It earns fees every time someone uses it, so we look at how much it earns versus what investors are paying for it. We combine up to three simple checks: (1) How busy is the network? More activity usually means more value. (2) Is the market cap cheap or expensive compared to the fees it earns? (3) What price would make sense given its size rank? The Speculative Fair Value is the average of whichever checks produced a sensible result.
forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (3.0%/yr): projected growth based on this token's quality score (3.6/10). Bull (9.0%/yr): adoption accelerates and network usage grows strongly. Bear (-37.0%/yr): adoption slows or competition erodes the token's position. The bear scenario is steeper than usual because survivability is only 40.0% — this token has weaker dilution control or holder value accrual, making it more vulnerable in a downturn.
forecast_meta.iv_selection_rationale: Bucket A (Cash-Flow Protocol): Up to 7 methods are available — Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, Holder Revenue, Metcalfe Network Value, Network Activity (NVT), Rank Benchmark, and Dilution Adjustment. Protocol Fee DCF, Fee/MC Multiple, and Holder Revenue are unavailable: this token has no DeFiLlama fee tracking. Valuation relies entirely on network activity and market positioning signals. Network Activity (NVT) did not contribute to the median: the implied fair value fell outside the 0.1x–10x price bounds (volume is very low or very high relative to circulating supply). Rank Benchmark anchors to the expected market cap for this token's rank tier, quality-adjusted and capped at 2× current price to avoid distortion. Dilution Adjustment did not fire: FDV/MC is within the acceptable range for this quality tier, so no dilution penalty was applied. Only 1 method contributed to the median — the valuation should be treated as a rough anchor, not a precise fair value. Methods producing values outside 0.01x–15x of current price are excluded as outliers. The mean of all qualifying per-token estimates becomes the Speculative Fair Value.
